member.name: Seema Malhotra
member.synopsis: Seema Malhotra is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Feltham and Heston , and has been an MP continually since 15 December 2011. She currently holds the Government posts of Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Department for Education) (Equalities), and Parliamentary Under-Secretary (Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Adult Education and Further Education: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what the (a) budget and (b) outturn spending were for (i) 16-19 learning, (ii) Adult Education Budget, (iii) Adult Skills Fund, (iv) free courses for jobs, (v) skills bootcamps, (vi) Multiply, (vii) Advanced Le
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: National Careers Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many 19-24 year olds not in employment, education or training who used the National Careers Service made positive progress in 2022-23.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: National Careers Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many people accessed the National Careers Service (a) through its website, (b) over the phone and (c) face-to-face in financial year 2022-23.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Advisory Services: Older Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many people have accessed the Midlife MOT website since its launch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: An enhanced digital Midlife MOT offer went live on 5th July 2023. The website is open to all, and between 5th July 2023 and 20th May 2024, there were around 43,000 sessions consisting of around 29,000 unique users who had accessed the new site. Please not...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sixth Form Education: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many funded enrolments there were on each of the courses for 16 to 19 year olds that will have funding withdrawn after 1 August 2024 in each financial year since 2021-22.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The exact information requested is not published. Data on the number of enrolments in the 2020/21 academic year, funded through 16 to 19 study programmes on each of the qualifications that will be defunded from 1 August 2024, is published here: https://ww...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Humanitarian Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, if he will hold discussions with his Israeli counterpart on taking steps (a) following recent and (b) to help prevent future attacks on aid convoys by Israeli settlers in the West Bank.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Attacks by extremists on aid convoys en route to Gaza are appalling. The Foreign Secretary raised his concerns with Israeli Minister Gantz on 16 May.On 3 May, the Foreign Secretary announced new sanctions on extremist groups and individuals for inciting a...

writtenQuestions.heading: UNRWA: Security
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, what recent discussions he has had with his Israeli counterpart on the security of the UNRWA headquarters in East Jerusalem.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The arson attack against UNRWA's headquarters in East Jerusalem was unacceptable. The perpetrators of this attack must be held to account. The UK Head of Mission, together with Heads of Mission in several partner countries, condemned the incident in a joi...

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Hearing Impairment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether she has had recent discussions with the Royal Association for Deaf people on the accessibility of GP appointments for deaf and hearing-impaired people.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ensuring that general practice appointments are accessible to everyone, including those who are deaf or hearing-impaired, is a top priority for the Government. Our Delivery Plan for Recovering Access to Primary Care, published in May 2023, outlines the im...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether her Department issues guidance to GPs on alternatives to phone-based booking systems for access to appointments for deaf and hearing-impaired people.

writtenQuestions.heading: Development Aid: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, what recent assessment he has made of the impact of UK Official Development Assistance spending on the resilience of primary and secondary school education in crisis-affected countries.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The FCDO supports resilient education systems through our investments through multilateral partners; bilateral programmes; and education policy influencing. This includes FCDO's position paper, 'Addressing the Climate, Environment, and Biodiversity Crises...

writtenQuestions.heading: Development Aid: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, how many girls have been supported by UK Official Development Assistance funding to (a) enter and (b) continue education.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Girls' education is a UK international development priority. We champion the right of every girl to access 12 years of quality education. Since 2015, UK Official Development Assistance has supported over 10 million girls to gain a decent education. The FC...

writtenQuestions.heading: Apprentices and T-levels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many businesses can be involved in the provision of (a) a T Level industry placement and (b) flexi-job apprenticeships.
writtenQuestions.answerText: T Levels offer students a mixture of classroom learning and ‘on-the-job’ experience during an industry placement of at least 315 hours (approximately 45 days). Industry placements can be split across a maximum of two employers to help deliver a breadth of...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Casualties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, whether he has had recent discussions with his Israeli counterpart on injuries received by (a) children and (b) civilian men of working age in Gaza hit by Israeli drone fire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The British Government has underlined the need for Israel to ensure effective deconfliction in Gaza. We want to see Israel take greater care to limit its operations to military targets, avoid harming civilians, in particular children, and destroying homes...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Drinking Water
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, whether he has had recent discussions with his Israeli counterpart on the potential merits of the resumption of the supply of fresh water in Gaza.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The situation in Gaza is desperate. Too many Palestinian civilians have been killed and there is an urgent need to get more aid to the people of Gaza to prevent a famine. Israel has made some progress in allowing more aid into Gaza, but more needs to be d...

writtenQuestions.heading: Vocational Guidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what recent steps she has taken to ensure careers advisors have knowledge of level 4 and 5 qualifications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Careers Service (NCS) provides free, up to date, impartial information, advice and guidance on careers, skills and the Labour Market in England. It helps customers make informed choices about their career and learning options, whatever their ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Further Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what recent steps she has taken to help increase engagement between employers and further education colleges.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department wants providers to continue to offer high-quality, relevant provision and to build upon the already fantastic work they do in partnership with local employers. The coming decade will see substantial economic change and as the economy change...
